name: aws-bedrock-for-copilot
description: Amazon Bedrock integration using VSCode's official Language Model Chat Provider API. Connects AWS Bedrock models such as Claude (including Opus 4.7), OpenAI OSS, DeepSeek to GitHub Copilot Chat via AWS SDK. Supports streaming, tool calling, and vision.

aws-bedrock-for-copilot.region.description: AWS region for Amazon Bedrock API calls. Bedrock model availability varies by region -- see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/models-regions.html

aws-bedrock-for-copilot.profile.description: AWS named profile from ~/.aws/credentials. Leave empty to use default credentials chain (environment variables, IAM roles, SSO, etc.)

aws-bedrock-for-copilot.preferredModel.description: Preferred Amazon Bedrock model ID to use by default (e.g., 'anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022-v2:0'). Leave empty to let Copilot choose.

aws-bedrock-for-copilot.anthropic.thinki.description: Enable extended thinking for supported Anthropic Claude models (Opus 4.x, Sonnet 4.x, Sonnet 3.7, Haiku 4.5). Allows models to reason through complex tasks before responding.

Tokens allocated for thinking on Claude models that use enabled+budget thinking (Opus 4.5, 4.1, 4, Sonnet 4.5, 4, 3.7, Haiku 4.5). For Claude Opus 4.6/4.7 and Sonnet 4.6 the budget is auto-managed via adaptive thinking and this setting is ignored.

enumDescriptions: Most efficient. Significant token savings with some capability reduction. Best for simpler tasks that need speed and lowest costs.
Balanced approach with moderate token savings. Good for tasks requiring a balance of speed, cost, and performance.
Maximum capability (default). Claude uses as many tokens as needed for excellent results. Recommended for complex reasoning and coding.
Extended capability for long-horizon agentic work (Opus 4.7 only). Falls back to 'high' on other models. Use with large max_tokens for coding/agentic tasks.
Absolute maximum capability with no constraints on token spending (Opus 4.6, 4.7, Sonnet 4.6 only). Falls back to 'high' on other models.
aws-bedrock-for-copilot.anthropic.thinki.description: Thinking effort level for adaptive-thinking Claude models (Opus 4.6, 4.7, Sonnet 4.6). Controls how eager Claude is about spending tokens. Some levels are model-gated; unsupported levels fall back to 'high'.

aws-bedrock-for-copilot.anthropic.contex.description: Enable 1M context window for Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6. (Opus 4.7 always uses 1M.) When enabled, these models can handle up to 1 million tokens of input context. Note: 1M context costs more.

aws-bedrock-for-copilot.anthropic.infere.description: Prefer regional inference profiles over global ones for Anthropic Claude models. Enable this if your policies restrict access to global inference profiles. When enabled, the extension uses regional profiles (e.g., us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-6-v1) instead of global (e.g., global.anthropic.claude-opus-4-6-v1).

enumDescriptions: Lowest reasoning, fastest responses (OpenAI gpt-oss only -- silently rejected by other vendors and falls back to 'low').
Default. Most thorough reasoning, best quality.
aws-bedrock-for-copilot.reasoningEffort.description: Reasoning effort sent as `reasoning_effort` for non-Anthropic models that support it (OpenAI gpt-oss, DeepSeek V3.2, Moonshot Kimi K2.5/K2 Thinking, Qwen3, Z.AI GLM, MiniMax M2.x). Silently ignored on models that don't support reasoning. Anthropic Claude uses its own `anthropic.thinking.effort` setting instead.

aws-bedrock-for-copilot.promptCaching.en.description: Enable prompt caching for supported models (Claude and Nova) to reduce latency and costs. Caches system prompts, tool definitions, and recent tool results. Disable if you need fully fresh responses or want to avoid cache-related costs.
